_**User**_

Explore the codebase at /Users/brendanoneill/data-maps to understand:

1. How data properties/items are typed - look for TypeScript interfaces/types that define the data model (likely in src/types/ or similar)
2. How "identifiable" status is represented in the data - search for "identifiable" across the codebase
3. What the possible values of identifiable status are (e.g., "identifiable.contact", "identifiable.personal", etc.)
4. How existing filters like sensitivity or classification work with the data to filter items

Be very thorough - search for "identifiable", "Identifiable", "IDENTIFIABLE" across all files. Also look at any mock data, seed data, or type definitions.

Return:
- All type/interface definitions related to data items
- All occurrences of "identifiable" in the codebase with file paths and context
- The possible identifiable values/categories
- How existing filters apply to data items (the filtering logic)

Here is a concise report based on the repo at `/Users/brendanoneill/data-maps`.

---

## 1. Type / interface definitions related to data items

**Primary data model** — `/Users/brendanoneill/data-maps/src/types/index.ts`:

- **`PrivacyDeclaration`**: `data_categories: string[]`, `data_subjects: string[]`, `data_use: string`, `name: string`
- **`System`**: `description`, `fides_key`, `name`, `privacy_declarations: PrivacyDeclaration[]`, `system_dependencies: string[]`, `system_type: string`

**Dashboard / filters** (same file):

- **`DimensionFilters`**: `systemType: string[]`, `dataUse: string[]`, `dataCategories: string[]`
- **`GroupedSystems`**: `key`, `label`, `systems: System[]`
- **`GroupByOption`**: `"systemType" | "dataUse" | "dataCategories"`
- **`GroupBySelection`**: `GroupByOption | "none"`

**Helpers** — `/Users/brendanoneill/data-maps/src/helpers/colors.ts`:

- **`ColorSet`**: `bg`, `border`, `text`, `dot`
- Internal **`ColorDimension`**: `"systemType" | "dataUse" | "dataCategories" | "fidesGroup"`

**Hooks** — `/Users/brendanoneill/data-maps/src/hooks/useFilteredSystems.ts`:

- **`UseFilteredSystemsParams`**, **`AvailableValues`**, **`UseFilteredSystemsResult`** (wrappers around `System`, filters, grouped output)

There is **no** dedicated TypeScript type for “identifiability,” “sensitivity,” or parsed Fides path segments. **`data_categories` entries are plain `string`s** (Fideslang-style dot paths).

---

## 2. How “identifiable” appears in the data

- It is **not** a separate field on `System` or `PrivacyDeclaration`.
- It is **embedded in each `data_categories[]` string** as the **third path segment** after `user` and `provided` / `derived`, e.g.  
  `user.derived.identifiable.device.cookie_id`.

**Parsing in code**: `classifyCategory()` in `/Users/brendanoneill/data-maps/src/helpers/categoryClassification.ts` scans path segments until it finds **`identifiable`** or **`nonidentifiable`**, then uses the **next** segment as the Fides group key (`device`, `contact`, etc.). It does **not** return or store “identifiable” as a value—only the group name.

---

## 3. All occurrences of “identifiable” (application + data; excluding `.specstory`)

| File | Context |
|------|--------|
| `/Users/brendanoneill/data-maps/sample_data.json` | Every `data_categories` value uses the substring `identifiable` in full paths (see below). |
| `/Users/brendanoneill/data-maps/src/helpers/categoryClassification.ts` | Docstring path template; example path; **`segments[i] === "identifiable" \|\| segments[i] === "nonidentifiable"`** to locate the group segment. |
| `/Users/brendanoneill/data-maps/src/helpers/formatLabel.ts` | Keys in `DATA_CATEGORY_LABELS` and doc example. |
| `/Users/brendanoneill/data-maps/src/helpers/colors.ts` | Keys in `DATA_CATEGORY_COLORS`. |
| `/Users/brendanoneill/data-maps/cypress/component/filter-bar.cy.tsx` | URL assertion containing `dc=user.derived.identifiable.device.cookie_id`. |

**`Identifiable` / `IDENTIFIABLE` (capitalized)**: No matches in `src/`, `cypress/`, or `sample_data.json`—only in `.specstory/history/` (planning notes), not in executable code.

---

## 4. Possible “identifiable” values / categories

**Path-level (Fideslang-style):**

- Documented alternatives for the third segment: **`identifiable`** | **`nonidentifiable`** (see `categoryClassification.ts` comment and branch).
- **Sample + label maps only use `identifiable`.** There are **no** `nonidentifiable` strings in `sample_data.json` or the static maps in `formatLabel.ts` / `colors.ts`.

**Concrete full category strings in this repo:**

From `formatLabel.ts` / `colors.ts` / `sample_data.json`:

- `user.derived.identifiable.device.cookie_id`
- `user.derived.identifiable.device.ip_address`
- `user.derived.identifiable.location`
- `user.provided.identifiable.contact.email`
- `user.provided.identifiable.financial`

**Not** separate enum values like `identifiable.contact` alone—the **full** category is always the dotted path; “contact” appears as **`...identifiable.contact.email`**.

**`FIDES_GROUP_MAP`** in `categoryClassification.ts` lists group keys that can appear **after** the identifiable segment: `device`, `contact`, `location`, `financial`, plus many others (credentials, health, biometric, etc.) for future/other datasets—mapped to display names like `"Device"`, `"Contact"`, …

**Sensitivity / “Sensitive” as a data field:** **Not implemented** in types or filters. `.specstory` history discusses a possible third tier (e.g. Sensitive via `isSensitive`); **no such field or filter exists in `src/`**.

---

## 5. How filters work (and how this relates to “classification” / “sensitivity”)

**State** (`useDashboardState.ts`): URL query params **`st`**, **`du`**, **`dc`** (comma-separated arrays) map to `DimensionFilters.systemType`, `.dataUse`, `.dataCategories`.

**Core logic** (`groupSystems.ts`):

1. **`groupAndFilterSystems`**: Starts from all systems, then **for each dimension that has selected values**, keeps systems that pass **`systemMatchesFilter`** for that dimension. Dimensions are combined with **AND**; within one dimension, selection is **OR** (any matching value keeps the system).

2. **`systemMatchesFilter`**:
   - **`systemType`**: `filterValues.includes(system.system_type)`
   - **`dataUse`**: some declaration has `filterValues.includes(d.data_use)`
   - **`dataCategories`**:
     - If **`fidesMode`** is true: some category **`c`** has `filterValues.includes(classifyCategory(c))` (compare **Fides group label**, e.g. `"Device"`, not the raw path).
     - If **`fidesMode`** is false: `filterValues.includes(c)` on the **raw** category string.

3. **Grouping** into swimlanes uses the same rules: for `dataCategories`, keys are either raw paths or `classifyCategory(c)` depending on `fidesMode`.

**`fidesMode`**: Stored in `localStorage` (`data-maps:fidesMode`). Turning it on **clears `dc`** (`setFidesMode`). It only changes how **`dataCategories`** are interpreted (rollup to Fides group names); it does **not** add sensitivity or identifiability filters.

**Natural-language / sentence UI** (`SentenceFilter.tsx`): Same three dimensions; no extra semantics.

---

### Summary for your four questions

| Topic | Finding |
|--------|--------|
| **Data item typing** | `System` / `PrivacyDeclaration` in `src/types/index.ts`; categories are **`string[]`**, not a discriminated union. |
| **Identifiable in data** | Substring / segment in each **`data_categories`** path; parsed only to find **Fides group**, not exposed as its own filter value. |
| **Possible values** | In this dataset: only **`...identifiable...`** paths; **`nonidentifiable`** is documented in code but unused in sample data. Five distinct full paths listed above. |
| **Filters vs “sensitivity/classification”** | Filters = **system type**, **data use**, **data categories** (raw or Fides group via **`fidesMode`**). **No** sensitivity or separate identifiability filter in the codebase. |
